title: Joshua: Land Allotment
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of joshua: land allotment and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study joshua: land allotment and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: How was the land of Canaan distributed among the tribes of Israel? Answer: by lot before the LORD at Shiloh.
segments.correct_answer: by lot before the LORD at Shiloh
acceptable_variants: casting lots at shiloh to determine each tribe's portion
they cast lots at the tabernacle in Shiloh for each tribal allotment
God determined the land boundaries through the lot at Shiloh

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The division of Canaan's land among the twelve tribes by lot in Joshua 13-21; the Levites received cities among the tribes rather than a tribal territory, since God was their inheritance
Six cities set aside in Joshua 20 where someone who killed accidentally could flee until a trial; they protected the innocent from blood vengeance
